title: nepeta involucrata
intro: Nepeta involucrata, also known as mint-leaved catmint, is a perennial flowering plant native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its grayish-green leaves and spikes of blue flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat digestive issues, nausea, and headaches, due to the presence of compounds such as flavonoids and iridoids. The health benefits of Nepeta involucrata include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory function, and act as an antispasmodic. Nepeta involucrata can be used in various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the leaves and flowers in hot water or a solvent.
